explanation: The natural language states that if 'New Patient' is selected, the user shall have to enter patient attributes and the completion of every step of the self-test procedure (FUN.6) shall be mandatory. Among the atomic propositions, 'newPatient' indicates 'New Patient' is selected, 'patientAttributesEntered' represents entering patient attributes, and 'selfTestPass' indicates all self-test steps passed successfully. The property requires BOTH entering patient attributes AND successful completion of self-test. Therefore, the appropriate translation is: always, if 'newPatient' then eventually both 'patientAttributesEntered' and 'selfTestPass' must occur. However, since only one proposition can be placed in bool_exp3, we combine them using the AND ( & ) operator: ('patientAttributesEntered' & 'selfTestPass'). The best fitting template is 'G (bool_exp1 -> (F bool_exp3))', where bool_exp1 is 'newPatient' and bool_exp3 is 'patientAttributesEntered & selfTestPass'. This ensures that whenever 'newPatient' is selected, eventually both the patient attributes will be entered and the self-test will be completed successfully.

The requirement specifies that if 'New Patient' is selected, two things must hold: 1) the user must enter patient attributes, and 2) the completion of every step of the self-test procedure (FUN.6) is mandatory. The atomic propositions 'newPatient', 'patientAttributesEntered', and 'selfTestPass' capture these concepts. The LTL expresses that globally, if 'newPatient' becomes true, then both 'patientAttributesEntered' and 'selfTestPass' must eventually become true. Since both are required, they are conjoined (using &) on the right-hand side; the formula enforces that whenever a new patient is selected, the patient attributes are entered and the self-test is completed. A reasonable template is 'G (bool_exp1 -> (F[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3))', which expresses that, whenever bool_exp1 ('newPatient') holds, it is required that bool_exp3 ('patientAttributesEntered & selfTestPass') will eventually hold within N_DURATION steps. You may want to tune N_DURATION based on UI requirements, but a typical integer like 5 suffices unless otherwise specified.

The required property states: Whenever 'newPatient' is selected, it must be that both 'patientAttributesEntered' and 'selfTestPass' are true. This captures the natural language: "If 'New Patient' is selected, the user shall have to enter patient attributes and the completion of every step of the self-test procedure (FUN.6) shall be mandatory." Here, 'newPatient' triggers the requirement, and 'patientAttributesEntered & selfTestPass' encodes that both actions must happen (attributes entered, self-test passes). The chosen template ensures that this must hold globally (at all times when 'newPatient' holds), as is common in requirements about mandatory procedures upon certain user actions. N_DURATION is set to 3 as a reasonable time bound for such user/system actions.

The property specifies that if 'New Patient' is selected, then both (a) the user must enter patient attributes, and (b) all steps of the self-test procedure must be completed (captured by 'selfTestPass'), which together express that both patient data entry and self-test step completion are mandatory before proceeding. These requirements are captured using 'newPatient' as the trigger condition, 'patientAttributesEntered' for the patient data entry, and 'selfTestPass' for the completion of all self-test steps. Both required actions are conjoined (ANDed), and the obligation is enforced globally (at all times) following the 'New Patient' selection. The template expresses this dependency concisely and unambiguously.

The requirement states: If 'New Patient' is selected, two things must happen: 1) the user must enter patient attributes, and 2) all steps of the self-test procedure must be completed (mandatory). In terms of atomic propositions, 'newPatient' encodes the selection, 'patientAttributesEntered' encodes entry of patient attributes, and 'selfTestPass' encodes completion of all self-test steps. We can express the requirement as: when 'newPatient' becomes true, both 'patientAttributesEntered' and 'selfTestPass' must become true (i.e., are required/constrained). The most direct LTL representation is: Globally, if 'newPatient' holds, then (eventually within a time bound) both patient attributes must be entered and the self-test must pass. Since the strongest requirement is that both must be achieved, I combine them with AND. This is best captured by template 34 (G (bool_exp1 -> (F[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3))), setting bool_exp1 to 'newPatient', and bool_exp3 to '(patientAttributesEntered & selfTestPass)'. N_DURATION is set to 10 as a reasonable finite timeframe for completion.

The natural language requirement specifies that when 'New Patient' is selected, two things must always happen: (1) the user must enter patient attributes, and (2) every step of the self-test procedure must be completed (implying the self-test must pass, i.e., 'selfTestPass' is true). The LTL property uses the template 'G (newPatient -> (patientAttributesEntered & selfTestPass))'. This means it is globally always true that if 'newPatient' occurs, then both 'patientAttributesEntered' and 'selfTestPass' must hold. This LTL property ensures both conditions are necessarily satisfied anytime 'New Patient' is selected.

The property expresses that whenever 'New Patient' is selected, it is always required that (1) patient attributes are entered and (2) all self-test steps are passed (no skipping), which captures both 'user shall have to enter patient attributes' and 'the completion of every step of the self-test procedure (FUN.6) shall be mandatory.' The formula enforces both conditions for each occurrence of newPatient, using the global (G) operator for all future states after selection.

The property states that whenever 'New Patient' is selected, it is always required that both patient attributes are entered and all self-test steps are passed successfully (which implies completion of each step). This is captured by requiring globally: if 'newPatient' holds, then both 'patientAttributesEntered' and 'selfTestPass' must also hold in the future. This enforces the mandatory entry of patient attributes and completion of every self-test step for new patient selection.

The property expresses that whenever 'New Patient' is selected (newPatient), it is always the case (globally) that after this, patient attributes must be entered (patientAttributesEntered), and every step of the self-test procedure must be completed, i.e., not skipping the steps for oxygen sensor, leaks, power switch, Fl2 flow meter, and local alarms. The template chosen ensures these conditions are always enforced following the selection of the 'New Patient' option. The boolean expression captures 'the user must enter patient attributes and cannot skip any required self-test step' as required by the natural language specification.

This LTL property formalizes the requirement that if 'New Patient' is selected, then two things must always be true: (1) patient attributes are entered, and (2) all self-test steps (i.e., selfTestPass) are mandatory (i.e., must be passed). We use template 37 (G (bool_exp1 -> (G bool_exp3))) with bool_exp1 as 'newPatient', and bool_exp3 as 'patientAttributesEntered & selfTestPass'. In this way, at all times, if a new patient is selected, it must always be the case that the patient attributes are entered and the self-test has been passed (no steps skipped). This matches the intent of the natural language specification.
